titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the James Dowling Trask Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 10:01

did.abstract: The papers consist of personal papers, memorabilia, photographs and medals of James Dowling Trask (1890-1942), his father and grandfather (1821-1883), also named James Dowling Trask. The bulk of the collection is related to James Dowling Trask (1890-1942).

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale University Historical Medical Library, 1980.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Dowling Trask Papers (MS 1339). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James Dowling Trask was born in Astoria, New York on August 21, 1890. After receiving his Ph.B. from Yale College in 1913, Trask went to Cornell Medical College from which he received an M.D. in 1917. Following service in the army and medical research, Trask returned to Yale in 1921 to become an instructor at the School of Medicine. That same year, he married Phyllis Hayden Randall, with whom he would have one daughter, also named Phyllis. Specializing in pediatrics, Trask focused his research on pneumonia, scarlet fever and streptococcal infections. He died in the spring of 1942 of septicemia and peritonitis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of personal papers, memorabilia, photographs and medals of James Dowling Trask (1890-1942), his father and grandfather (1821-1883), also named James Dowling Trask. The bulk of the collection is related to James Dowling Trask (1890-1942).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged by family member.

did.unittitle: Clippings on polio and Trask's death
Cornell University Medical College, notebooks on bacteriology and clinical laboratory methods (2 notebooks)
Correspondence, including with Julia H. Trask (Trask's mother)
Correspondence relating to Dr. Trask's death
Grace-New Haven Community Hospital memorial opportunities booklet
Juvenalia, sketchbook and reports from Lawrenceville School
Liberty ship James D. Trask, correspondence, photographs, and printed ephemera
Long Island College of Medicine anniversary
Photographs, mostly portraits of James Dowling Trask
Printed ephemera, passports, memberships, and other documents,
Scarlet Fever by James D. Trask
Sheffield Scientific School, Yale University, grades and class bulletins
War and medical decorations and realia
World War II, War Department correspondence and orders
Yale Journal of Biology and Medicine , James Dowling Trask Memorial Number (vol. 16, no. 5)
Financial records, correspondence, and printed ephemera relating to his medical practice
James Dowling Trask (Senior) and James Dowling Trask (1820-1883), public health and medical practice manuscripts and memorabilia
Public health and medical practice manuscripts and memorabilia
